name: Barbell Upright Row
resistance_profile: Standing compound pull; torque on the delts is greatest through the mid-range as the elbows travel out and up toward shoulder height.
cues: Lead with the elbows and keep them above the wrists
Pull only to shoulder height, not to the chin
Use a wider grip to favour the side delts over the traps
execution_steps: Grip a loaded barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms hanging straight, the bar resting against the thighs.
Stand tall with a braced core and let the shoulders relax down.
Lead with the elbows and pull the bar up along the body, keeping the elbows higher than the wrists.
Stop when the upper arms reach about shoulder height (elbows roughly level with the shoulders).
Lower the bar under control back to the thighs and repeat.
good_when: You want a compound movement that trains the side delts and upper traps together
You want to load the side delts heavier than isolation raises allow
bad_when: You have a history of shoulder impingement or the movement pinches
You cannot keep the elbows leading and the wrists take over
common_errors: Pulling too high toward the chin and pinching the shoulder
Using a narrow grip that shifts the work to the traps
Heaving with the lower back and momentum
progressions: Pause at the top for a peak-contraction hold
regressions: Switch to a cable or dumbbells for a friendlier bar path
Reduce the range and pull only to lower-chest height
